rulings.Case Title: PAWNEE LEASING CORPORATION VS. HITESH INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Fix Attorneys Fees As An Element Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 09, 2024, line 1, PLAINTIFF PAWNEE LEASING CORPORATION'S Motion To Fix Attorneys Fees As An Element Of Costs Plaintiff's "motion to fix attorneys fees as an element of costs" in the amount of $13,070 is granted; no timely opposition filed. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: YEUZI XU VS. YI CHEN CHIEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For A New Trial And Motion To Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 09, 2024, line 3, DEFENDANT CHIEN YI-CHEN Motion For A New Trial And Motion To Vacate Judgment Ordered off calendar; no proof of service filed in register of actions. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: REGENT WEST LTD., L.P., A CALIFORNIA VS. FRANK P. REYES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Monday, December 9, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANT FRANK REYES's Motion For Protective Order. Defendants' motion for protective order is ordered off calendar to give counsel an opportunity to meaningfully meet and confer. These disputes regard 412 discovery demands, but the meet-and-confer was limited to one terse e-mail exchange. The new meet-and-confer shall be conducted in person and Timothy Travelstead and Samer Danfoura shall participate in it. After full compliance with the court's order, the protective order motion may be re-set (if necessary). =(302/RBU)
